items.claim: Conor McGregor is no longer the same person he was 10 years ago.
items.quote: From From the first 10 years ago when we were interviewing him, he was very, very different to where he is right now.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's long layoff makes it impossible for him to compete with someone as capable as Max Holloway.
items.quote: Nobody can take 10 years off at least 5 years off from this sport and live with somebody as capable as Max Holloway.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has a significant advantage moving up to 170 pounds.
Conor McGregor will knock out Max Holloway in the first round if he is to win.
items.quote: If Conor gets it done, it's the first round. And if he does it, he'll knock him out in a spectacular fashion.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's cardio fades quickly, even in his prime.
items.quote: even in his prime, he wasn't known for his cardio... He's very explosive. He's very fast and he starts to fade quickly, rather quickly.

items.claim: Conor McGregor cannot fix his historical cardio issues after such a long time away from the sport.
items.quote: If he couldn't address that in 2013, 14, 15 when he was a double champion of the world, after all this time away, I don't see how he can fix that now.

items.claim: Max Holloway has a superior legacy to Conor McGregor due to his consistency in defending titles and taking on rematches.
items.quote: Max stuck around, defended those belts, took on big challenges. Rematches... Conor never defended the belts.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's lack of training camps and sparring during his layoff puts him at a disadvantage compared to active fighters.
items.quote: he hasn't been going through training camps, he hasn't been getting ragged around on the floor and people pulling on his neck and taking shots in sparring.

items.claim: Dominick Cruz was no longer the same fighter after his injuries and had to completely change his fighting style.
items.quote: Dominick Cruz, after he came back from all his injuries, was no longer Dominick Cruz. Couldn't move the way he used to move... he had to completely change his fighting style.
